title: Executive Order 10251—Suspension of the Eight-Hour Law as to Laborers and Mechanics Employed by the Department of Defense on Public Work Essential to the National Defense

flags.justification: Invokes a specific, narrow, statute-provided emergency-suspension mechanism (the 1892/1913 Act's own built-in exception) rather than creating new discretionary authority.
A clear, single, defined-scope action limited to Department of Defense public work.
Single purpose: suspending one specific labor-hour limitation.

Includes a specific, protective proviso: wages 'computed on a basic day rate of eight hours... with overtime... at time and one-half' — a concrete worker protection built directly into the suspension.
The explicit overtime-pay preservation forecloses any incentive to exploit extended hours without compensation.

Explicitly self-limits 'for the duration of the national emergency proclaimed... on December 16, 1950' — a genuine, functioning link to the underlying emergency's own resolution, not an indefinite continuation mechanism.
Exercises an already-existing, statute-provided suspension mechanism for its intended purpose; no independent oversight is removed.
The overtime-pay requirement provides a real, enforceable compliance standard.

The order itself notes it supersedes and extends several prior similar suspension orders (EO 9898, 9926, 9974, 10064, 10135), demonstrating this exact mechanism has been used routinely and repeatedly across multiple prior emergencies.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. A well-designed, narrow, protective emergency-suspension order with a genuine, functioning tie to the underlying emergency's resolution.
